one_time_description: Northwestern Ontario Schools Bundle Project - ownership of new school transferred from Shibogama Tribal Council to Shoal Lake 40 First Nation upon project completion
economic_notes: First Nation located in both Ontario and Manitoba. In June 1989 entered into 60-year agreement with Province of Manitoba and City of Winnipeg for preservation of water quality on Shoal Lake; each paid $3,000,000 to a Trustee for investment. Water treatment centre completed and put into use during the year at cost of $32,090,333. Housing projects under construction with estimated $1,000,000 to complete. ISC funding represents 16% of revenue (down from 45% in 2021). Investments in Kakina Economic Development Group (10%), Niiwin Wendaanimok Management Inc. (25%), Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022). Large one-time item: $17,752,945 school asset transferred from Shibogama Tribal Council to First Nation on project completion. Nation straddles Ontario/Manitoba border. Province set as ON (Kenora area). Consolidated entity includes CMHC Housing. Kekekoziibii Development Corporation is related entity but not consolidated (due from balance only). Schedule of Expenses by Object was cut off in source text - salaries figure of $4,208,815 extracted from partial data. No schedule of remuneration provided. Capital segment expenses of $10,121,646 include the school transfer and housing construction. Accounts payable includes $7,747,710 owing to Province of Manitoba. Annual debt service reflects only CMHC and vehicle loan principal repayments of $59,539 per cash flow statement; many ISC loans are contingent on claim settlements. OSR calculation is approximate as some revenue lines (OFNLP, investment income, etc.) could be considered transfers.
